title: CatBoost Ordered-Categorical Blend
summary: The best runs rely on AutoGluon GBM/XGB, while CatBoost is designed for categorical feature combinations and ordered target statistics.
rationale: The best runs rely on AutoGluon GBM/XGB, while CatBoost is designed for categorical feature combinations and ordered target statistics. Race, Driver, Compound, and engineered interaction categories are central here.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoostClassifier with native categorical columns plus the best engineered features, using ordered boosting, class weights or scale_pos_weight, and grouped validation by Race-Year for model selection. Blend its rank-normalized predictions with the current AutoGluon GBM/XGB predictions, optimizing blend weight on OOF AUC.
expected_effect: Adds model-family diversity and stronger categorical interaction handling without rewriting the feature pipeline.
risk: CatBoost can overfit high-cardinality Race/Driver interactions if validation remains random; rank blending helps AUC but calibration may not improve.
